shared_output_contract_assertions: Does the output include an explicit `Objective` section?
Does the output include a `Source Check` or `Tool Access Check` section with sources used and gaps?
Does the output include a `Current Signal` section that states what needs attention now?
Does the output include actions with item, owner, next action, due/review date, status, and evidence/source?
Does the output include unresolved details shown as `TBD`?
Does the output list next lane follow-ups without executing multiple lanes unless asked?

evals.prompt: Pipa find-to-define check: we found a promising lead from notes. Confirm if we have enough to define the work and list the exact missing inputs if not.
evals.expected_output: Validates whether opportunity evidence, buyer pain, constraints, and decision context are sufficient to move from find-work into define-work.
assertions: Does the output explicitly evaluate opportunity evidence and why-now signal?
Does it evaluate buyer or stakeholder context?
Does it evaluate known constraints needed for a working brief?
If evidence is incomplete, does it mark the handoff as blocked or partial with only the minimum required inputs?
Does it satisfy all shared output contract assertions listed in this suite?

evals.prompt: Pipa define-to-deliver check: before we start delivery this week, validate whether the brief is execution-ready. If something is missing, keep the ask minimal and specific.
evals.expected_output: Checks define-work readiness: scope, out-of-scope, acceptance checks, owner/action/date/status readiness, and decision gaps.
assertions: Does the output explicitly evaluate scope and out-of-scope clarity?
Does it evaluate acceptance checks?
Does it evaluate owners, next actions, and timing readiness?
If gate evidence is incomplete, does it mark blocked or partial with the smallest next input set needed to unblock?
Does it satisfy all shared output contract assertions listed in this suite?

evals.prompt: Pipa deliver-to-get-paid check: we are mid-delivery and need to know whether anything affects invoicing, budget, billable time, or change control.
evals.expected_output: Checks whether delivery state contains money-impacting signals and routes only those signals into get-paid follow-up.
assertions: Does the output identify delivery items with payment, budget, billable time, margin, or change-control impact?
Does it distinguish known money facts from `TBD` gaps?
Does it avoid claiming invoice or payment access without connected tools?
Does it satisfy all shared output contract assertions listed in this suite?

evals.prompt: Pipa deliver-to-relationships check: identify any client or stakeholder relationship risks from this delivery status before we send updates.
evals.expected_output: Surfaces relationship-sensitive delivery signals and recommends the smallest relationship follow-up without sending anything.
assertions: Does the output identify relationship risks or check-in opportunities from delivery evidence?
Does it recommend a suggested touch with owner, timing, and source?
Does it avoid scheduling reminders or sending messages unless explicitly asked?
Does it satisfy all shared output contract assertions listed in this suite?

evals.transition: learn from the work -> grow relationships
evals.prompt: Pipa learn-to-relationships check: from this closeout, what relationship follow-up should happen next and what evidence supports it?
evals.expected_output: Turns closeout lessons into a relationship follow-up or watchlist item with owner, timing, and evidence.
assertions: Does the output extract a lesson or closeout signal with evidence?
Does it translate that signal into a relationship next action or watchlist item?
Does it preserve missing owner, timing, or source details as `TBD`?
Does it satisfy all shared output contract assertions listed in this suite?
